description: Complete reusable content infrastructure for the ARES EDU PLATFORM. All content engines are domain-agnostic and support unlimited future educational content.

alp.name: ARES Learning Package
alp.description: Modular package architecture for educational products

book_engine.name: Book Engine
book_engine.description: Complete book architecture: volumes, parts, chapters, sections, appendices, glossary, indexes, version history, translations

course_engine.name: Course Engine
course_engine.description: Course architecture: modules, units, projects, labs, assessments, certificates, prerequisites

content_management.name: Content Management Engine
content_management.description: Content lifecycle: draft, review, published, archived, deprecated, versioning, revision history, change tracking

localization.name: Localization Engine
localization.description: Multilingual support: Arabic, English, Finnish, Kurdish + future. RTL/LTR, translation memory, machine translation

media.name: Media Engine
media.description: Asset management: images, SVG, icons, Mermaid, video, audio, interactive diagrams, PDF, downloads

interactive_blocks.name: Interactive Block Engine
interactive_blocks.description: Reusable interactive blocks: quizzes, exercises, decision trees, code blocks, terminal blocks, flashcards, callouts

question.name: Question Engine
question.description: 12 question types, 6 bank types, 6 scoring strategies, rubrics, certificates, AI integration

assessment.name: Assessment Engine
assessment.description: 9 assessment types, 4 grading approaches, feedback modes, analytics, anti-cheating

export.name: Export Engine
export.description: 12 export formats: Markdown, MDX, HTML, PDF, ePub, JSON, offline, print, CSV, YAML, slides

import.name: Import Engine
import.description: 11 import formats, 6-stage pipeline, content deduplication, batch import

search.name: Content Search Engine
search.description: Full-text, faceted, semantic, knowledge graph search across all content types

analytics.name: Content Analytics Engine
analytics.description: Usage, completion, difficulty, popularity, search, question, book, course analytics with AI insights

author_system.name: Author System
author_system.description: 8 author roles, 5 organization types, 4 workflows, attribution, licensing, reputation

design_decisions: ALP is the atomic unit of content packaging — everything installable is an ALP
All engines are domain-agnostic — no references to 'cloud', 'engineering', or any specific subject
Books support volumes → parts → chapters → sections → lessons hierarchy
Courses support modules → units → lessons with embedded projects/labs/assessments
5 content lifecycle states: draft → review → published → deprecated → archived
Localization supports RTL (Arabic, Kurdish) and LTR languages
Question engine supports 12 question types with 6 bank types
Export supports 12 formats; Import supports 11 formats with 6-stage pipeline
Search supports full-text + faceted + semantic + knowledge graph modes
Author system supports 8 roles with 4 distinct workflows
All schemas are JSON Schema compliant; all types are fully typed in TypeScript
extension_points: Custom ALP module types via ALPExtension
Custom question types via plugin system
Custom export/import formats via plugin system
Custom search providers (local, algolia, typesense, meilisearch, elasticsearch)
Custom assessment types and grading strategies
External identity integration (ORCID, Google Scholar, LinkedIn)
next_phase: Phase 5 — Content & Community (actual educational content creation, community features, marketplace)
